revision.text:
{{Infobox mountain | Name = Kamuro-yama (神室山) | Image = [[File:Kamuroyama.jpg|300px]] | Caption = Kamuro-yama and an emergency shelter (August 2011) | Elevation = 1365.2m | Coordinates = {{Coord|38|54|8|N|140|29|32.3|E|type:mountain_region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Location = {{flag|Japan}}
{{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県}} (Yuzawa City), {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}} (Shinjō City, Kaneyama Town) | Range = Kamuro Range | Type = Fault-block mountain ([[Granite]]) | First Ascent = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|38.902230|140.492306}}|caption=|width=300}}Location of Kamuro-yama }} '''Kamuro-yama''' (神室山) is a mountain that straddles the borders of {{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県}} (Yuzawa City) and {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}} (Shinjō City and Kaneyama Town), serving as the main peak of the Kamuro Range. It is one of the '''{{ill|200 Famous Japanese Mountains|ja|日本二百名山}}'''. This article also describes the Kamuro Range (Kamuro Mountain Range) in general. == Overview == The Kamuro Range, with Kamuro-yama as its main peak, stretches for over 30 km, comprising peaks such as {{ill|Kuro-mori|ja|黒森}} (1,057 m), {{ill|Suishō-mori|ja|水晶森}} (1,097 m), {{ill|Mae-Kamuro-yama|ja|前神室山}} (1,342 m), Kamuro-yama (1,365 m), {{ill|Tengu-mori|ja|天狗森}} (1,302 m), {{ill|Komata-yama|ja|小又山}} (1,366 m), {{ill|Hiuchi-dake|ja|火打岳}} (1,237 m), {{ill|Hachihori-yama|ja|八森山}} (1,098 m), {{ill|Mokuzō-yama|ja|杢蔵山}} (1,026 m), and {{ill|Ōmori-yama|ja|大森山}} (591 m). Although not exceptionally tall, its rugged appearance, sculpted by heavy snowfall, has earned it the nickname "The Mini-Alps of Tōhoku." The entire range is part of the {{ill|Kurikoma Quasi-National Park|ja|栗駒国定公園}}. Kamuro-yama has been a site of {{ill|mountain worship|ja|山岳信仰}} since ancient times, comparable to {{ill|Mount Chōkai|ja|鳥海山}} as a sacred mountain for ascetic practices. According to legend, it was opened by {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂}} during his eastern expeditions. The mountain has strong ties to Mount Chōkai, sharing the same deity, {{ill|Ōmonoimi no Kami|ja|大物忌神}}. It was revered as a deity of water and agriculture, with people climbing the mountain to pray for abundant harvests. Stone markers inscribed with names like "Kamuro Gongen," "Raijin," "Ōta-no-Kami," and "Suijin" remain on the summit, along with several sites thought to have been used for rituals, such as scattering rice to pray for bountiful harvests. Legends like "The Tengu of Kamuro-yama" also abound. Traces of pilgrim accommodations remain in the surrounding villages, such as Yamazaki Warabō in Kaneyama Town. Temples in the region also reflect the mountain's significance in {{ill|Shugendō|ja|修験道}}. Although Shugendō practices at Kamuro-yama declined due to concerns over moral corruption among monks, the mountain remains a site of local reverence. Despite its moderate elevation, heavy snowfall allows alpine plants to thrive at lower altitudes. Well-maintained trails make it a popular destination for hikers. == Hiking Information == * The emergency shelter at the summit was closed due to deterioration but was rebuilt over two years starting in 2009. It reopened in October 2010. A water source is located near the shelter. * Other water sources are limited to a few locations: near the Kamuro-yama shelter, Mokuzō Mountain Lodge, and the trail junction at "Sariguchi" between Komata-yama and Hiuchi-dake. * The ridges are narrow, posing a high risk of slipping. * The annual opening of the Kamuro Range in Shinjō City takes place in early June. [[File:Kamurosan Santyô.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Summit of Kamuro-yama (August 2011)]] == Access == === Akita Prefecture (Yuzawa City) === * '''Yakunai Trailhead''' ** '''Nishinokuchi Route''': This route follows Nishinokuchi River to the summit, featuring stone statues of Fudō Myōō, steep climbs like "Munazuka Hatchō," and scenic wetlands at Onta-no-Kami. ** '''Panorama Route''': This route passes Mae-Kamuro-yama and joins the Araya Route, featuring Kabutayama Shrine and scenic viewpoints. === Yamagata Prefecture (Kaneyama Town) === * '''Suishō-mori Trailhead''': Once a major route through the {{ill|Dewa Province|ja|出羽国}}, this trail leads to Suishō-mori and Mae-Kamuro-yama. * '''Araya Trailhead''': A route along Ōtaki Gorge leading to the summit. === Yamagata Prefecture (Shinjō City) === * '''Donai Trailhead''': This route joins the Kamazawa Trail and ascends via the Daiyama Ridge. * '''Sariguchi Trailhead''': A path through Donai Gorge to Sariguchi. * '''Hiuchi Shinmichi Trailhead''': Directly ascends Hiuchi-dake. * '''Hagino Trailhead''': A route to Hachihori-yama via Ippaimori. * '''San’ya Trailhead''': Passes through Mokuzō-yama and connects to other ridge trails. === Yamagata Prefecture (Mogami Town) === * '''Yakushihara Trailhead''': Ascends to Hachihori-yama. * '''Oyagurumi Trailhead''': Leads to the ridge between Komata-yama and Hachihori-yama. * '''Nishinokuchi Trailhead''': Starts near Shirakawa Dam and ascends Komata-yama. == Gallery == 三十三尋の滝２.jpg|Sanju-sanjihiro Falls on the Nishinokuchi Route 胸突八丁坂２.jpg|Munazuka Hatchō slope on the Nishinokuchi Route クマのひるね坂から御田の神.jpg|Onta-no-Kami seen from Kuma no Hirune slope 前神室山と雄勝・秋ノ宮の集落２.jpg|Mae-Kamuro-yama with Ogatsu and Akinomiya settlements 神室山山頂２.jpg|Summit of Kamuro-yama 避難小屋と雲海の広がる新庄盆地２.jpg|Shelter and sea of clouds over the Shinjō Basin (Gassan in the background) 栗駒山と虎毛山２.jpg|{{ill|Mount Kurikoma|ja|栗駒山}} (left) and {{ill|Torage-yama|ja|虎毛山}} (right) 前神室山.jpg|Mae-Kamuro-yama == Supplementary Notes == * Nearby is another mountain called {{ill|Karekake-dake|ja|禿岳}}, also pronounced "Kamuro-san." According to local lore, the two are considered sisters. * Surrounding sacred mountains include Kaneyama's {{ill|Ryūma-yama|ja|竜馬山}}, Mogami's Gongen-yama, Shinjō's Hachihori-yama, and the "Rokushō-dake" group in the {{ill|Kammu Prefectural Nature Park|ja|加無山県立自然公園}}. == External Links == * [https://maps.gsi.go.jp/#15/38.902230/140.492306/&base=std&ls=std&disp=1&vs=c1j0h0k0l0u0t0z0r0s0m0f1 Topographic Map of Kamuro-yama (Geospatial Information Authority of Japan)] * [https://www.city.shinjo.yamagata.jp/k001/020/050/150/20150313172806.html Kamuro-yama Shelter Information (Shinjō City Official Website)] * [http://www.ecopolis.or.jp/kanko/ Mogami Regional Tourism Council] {{200 Famous Japanese Mountains}} {{100 Famous Tōhoku Mountains}} {{Mountain-stub|pref=Akita Prefecture|pref2=Yamagata Prefecture}} [[Category:Mountain ranges in Japan]] [[Category:Mountains of Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Mountains of Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:Scenic spots in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Scenic spots in Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinjō City]] [[Category:Geography of Yuzawa City]] [[Category:Kaneyama, Yamagata]] [[Category:Mountains of the Ōu Range]] [[Category:1000-meter mountains]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:Kamuro-yama]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:Kamuro-yama]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[ja:神室山]] [[Category:Omonoimi faith]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] {{Omonoimi Faith}}

According to local lore, the two are considered sisters. * Surrounding sacred mountains include Kaneyama's {{ill|Ryūma-yama|ja|竜馬山}}, Mogami's Gongen-yama, Shinjō's Hachihori-yama, and the "Rokushō-dake" group in the {{ill|Kammu Prefectural Nature Park|ja|加無山県立自然公園}}. == External Links == * [https://maps.gsi.go.jp/#15/38.902230/140.492306/&base=std&ls=std&disp=1&vs=c1j0h0k0l0u0t0z0r0s0m0f1 Topographic Map of Kamuro-yama (Geospatial Information Authority of Japan)] * [https://www.city.shinjo.yamagata.jp/k001/020/050/150/20150313172806.html Kamuro-yama Shelter Information (Shinjō City Official Website)] * [http://www.ecopolis.or.jp/kanko/ Mogami Regional Tourism Council] {{200 Famous Japanese Mountains}} {{100 Famous Tōhoku Mountains}} {{Mountain-stub|pref=Akita Prefecture|pref2=Yamagata Prefecture}} [[Category:Mountain ranges in Japan]] [[Category:Mountains of Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Mountains of Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:Scenic spots in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Scenic spots in Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinjō City]] [[Category:Geography of Yuzawa City]] [[Category:Kaneyama, Yamagata]] [[Category:Mountains of the Ōu Range]] [[Category:1000-meter mountains]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:Kamuro-yama]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:Kamuro-yama]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[ja:神室山]] [[Category:Omonoimi faith]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] {{Omonoimi Faith}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox mountain | Name = Kamuro-yama (神室山) | Image = [[File:Kamuroyama.jpg|300px]] | Caption = Kamuro-yama and an emergency shelter (August 2011) | Elevation = 1365.2m | Coordinates = {{Coord|38|54|8|N|140|29|32.3|E|type:mountain_region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Location = {{flag|Japan}}
{{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県}} (Yuzawa City), {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}} (Shinjō City, Kaneyama Town) | Range = Kamuro Range | Type = Fault-block mountain ([[Granite]]) | First Ascent = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|38.902230|140.492306}}|caption=|width=300}}Location of Kamuro-yama }} '''Kamuro-yama''' (神室山) is a mountain that straddles the borders of {{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県}} (Yuzawa City) and {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}} (Shinjō City and Kaneyama Town), serving as the main peak of the Kamuro Range. It is one of the '''{{ill|200 Famous Japanese Mountains|ja|日本二百名山}}'''. This article also describes the Kamuro Range (Kamuro Mountain Range) in general. == Overview == The Kamuro Range, with Kamuro-yama as its main peak, stretches for over 30 km, comprising peaks such as {{ill|Kuro-mori|ja|黒森}} (1,057 m), {{ill|Suishō-mori|ja|水晶森}} (1,097 m), {{ill|Mae-Kamuro-yama|ja|前神室山}} (1,342 m), Kamuro-yama (1,365 m), {{ill|Tengu-mori|ja|天狗森}} (1,302 m), {{ill|Komata-yama|ja|小又山}} (1,366 m), {{ill|Hiuchi-dake|ja|火打岳}} (1,237 m), {{ill|Hachihori-yama|ja|八森山}} (1,098 m), {{ill|Mokuzō-yama|ja|杢蔵山}} (1,026 m), and {{ill|Ōmori-yama|ja|大森山}} (591 m). Although not exceptionally tall, its rugged appearance, sculpted by heavy snowfall, has earned it the nickname "The Mini-Alps of Tōhoku." The entire range is part of the {{ill|Kurikoma Quasi-National Park|ja|栗駒国定公園}}. Kamuro-yama has been a site of {{ill|mountain worship|ja|山岳信仰}} since ancient times, comparable to {{ill|Mount Chōkai|ja|鳥海山}} as a sacred mountain for ascetic practices. According to legend, it was opened by {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂}} during his eastern expeditions. The mountain has strong ties to Mount Chōkai, sharing the same deity, {{ill|Ōmonoimi no Kami|ja|大物忌神}}. It was revered as a deity of water and agriculture, with people climbing the mountain to pray for abundant harvests. Stone markers inscribed with names like "Kamuro Gongen," "Raijin," "Ōta-no-Kami," and "Suijin" remain on the summit, along with several sites thought to have been used for rituals, such as scattering rice to pray for bountiful harvests. Legends like "The Tengu of Kamuro-yama" also abound. Traces of pilgrim accommodations remain in the surrounding villages, such as Yamazaki Warabō in Kaneyama Town. Temples in the region also reflect the mountain's significance in {{ill|Shugendō|ja|修験道}}. Although Shugendō practices at Kamuro-yama declined due to concerns over moral corruption among monks, the mountain remains a site of local reverence. Despite its moderate elevation, heavy snowfall allows alpine plants to thrive at lower altitudes. Well-maintained trails make it a popular destination for hikers. == Hiking Information == * The emergency shelter at the summit was closed due to deterioration but was rebuilt over two years starting in 2009. It reopened in October 2010. A water source is located near the shelter. * Other water sources are limited to a few locations: near the Kamuro-yama shelter, Mokuzō Mountain Lodge, and the trail junction at "Sariguchi" between Komata-yama and Hiuchi-dake. * The ridges are narrow, posing a high risk of slipping. * The annual opening of the Kamuro Range in Shinjō City takes place in early June. [[File:Kamurosan Santyô.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Summit of Kamuro-yama (August 2011)]] == Access == === Akita Prefecture (Yuzawa City) === * '''Yakunai Trailhead''' ** '''Nishinokuchi Route''': This route follows Nishinokuchi River to the summit, featuring stone statues of Fudō Myōō, steep climbs like "Munazuka Hatchō," and scenic wetlands at Onta-no-Kami. ** '''Panorama Route''': This route passes Mae-Kamuro-yama and joins the Araya Route, featuring Kabutayama Shrine and scenic viewpoints. === Yamagata Prefecture (Kaneyama Town) === * '''Suishō-mori Trailhead''': Once a major route through the {{ill|Dewa Province|ja|出羽国}}, this trail leads to Suishō-mori and Mae-Kamuro-yama. * '''Araya Trailhead''': A route along Ōtaki Gorge leading to the summit. === Yamagata Prefecture (Shinjō City) === * '''Donai Trailhead''': This route joins the Kamazawa Trail and ascends via the Daiyama Ridge. * '''Sariguchi Trailhead''': A path through Donai Gorge to Sariguchi. * '''Hiuchi Shinmichi Trailhead''': Directly ascends Hiuchi-dake. * '''Hagino Trailhead''': A route to Hachihori-yama via Ippaimori. * '''San’ya Trailhead''': Passes through Mokuzō-yama and connects to other ridge trails. === Yamagata Prefecture (Mogami Town) === * '''Yakushihara Trailhead''': Ascends to Hachihori-yama. * '''Oyagurumi Trailhead''': Leads to the ridge between Komata-yama and Hachihori-yama. * '''Nishinokuchi Trailhead''': Starts near Shirakawa Dam and ascends Komata-yama. == Gallery == 三十三尋の滝２.jpg|Sanju-sanjihiro Falls on the Nishinokuchi Route 胸突八丁坂２.jpg|Munazuka Hatchō slope on the Nishinokuchi Route クマのひるね坂から御田の神.jpg|Onta-no-Kami seen from Kuma no Hirune slope 前神室山と雄勝・秋ノ宮の集落２.jpg|Mae-Kamuro-yama with Ogatsu and Akinomiya settlements 神室山山頂２.jpg|Summit of Kamuro-yama 避難小屋と雲海の広がる新庄盆地２.jpg|Shelter and sea of clouds over the Shinjō Basin (Gassan in the background) 栗駒山と虎毛山２.jpg|{{ill|Mount Kurikoma|ja|栗駒山}} (left) and {{ill|Torage-yama|ja|虎毛山}} (right) 前神室山.jpg|Mae-Kamuro-yama == Supplementary Notes == * Nearby is another mountain called {{ill|Karekake-dake|ja|禿岳}}, also pronounced "Kamuro-san." According to local lore, the two are considered sisters. * Surrounding sacred mountains include Kaneyama's {{ill|Ryūma-yama|ja|竜馬山}}, Mogami's Gongen-yama, Shinjō's Hachihori-yama, and the "Rokushō-dake" group in the {{ill|Kammu Prefectural Nature Park|ja|加無山県立自然公園}}. == External Links == * [https://maps.gsi.go.jp/#15/38.902230/140.492306/&base=std&ls=std&disp=1&vs=c1j0h0k0l0u0t0z0r0s0m0f1 Topographic Map of Kamuro-yama (Geospatial Information Authority of Japan)] * [https://www.city.shinjo.yamagata.jp/k001/020/050/150/20150313172806.html Kamuro-yama Shelter Information (Shinjō City Official Website)] * [http://www.ecopolis.or.jp/kanko/ Mogami Regional Tourism Council] {{200 Famous Japanese Mountains}} {{100 Famous Tōhoku Mountains}} {{Mountain-stub|pref=Akita Prefecture|pref2=Yamagata Prefecture}} [[Category:Mountain ranges in Japan]] [[Category:Mountains of Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Mountains of Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:Scenic spots in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Scenic spots in Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinjō City]] [[Category:Geography of Yuzawa City]] [[Category:Kaneyama, Yamagata]] [[Category:Mountains of the Ōu Range]] [[Category:1000-meter mountains]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:Kamuro-yama]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:Kamuro-yama]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[ja:神室山]] [[Category:Omonoimi faith]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] {{Omonoimi Faith}}

According to local lore, the two are considered sisters. * Surrounding sacred mountains include Kaneyama's {{ill|Ryūma-yama|ja|竜馬山}}, Mogami's Gongen-yama, Shinjō's Hachihori-yama, and the "Rokushō-dake" group in the {{ill|Kammu Prefectural Nature Park|ja|加無山県立自然公園}}. == External Links == * [https://maps.gsi.go.jp/#15/38.902230/140.492306/&base=std&ls=std&disp=1&vs=c1j0h0k0l0u0t0z0r0s0m0f1 Topographic Map of Kamuro-yama (Geospatial Information Authority of Japan)] * [https://www.city.shinjo.yamagata.jp/k001/020/050/150/20150313172806.html Kamuro-yama Shelter Information (Shinjō City Official Website)] * [http://www.ecopolis.or.jp/kanko/ Mogami Regional Tourism Council] {{200 Famous Japanese Mountains}} {{100 Famous Tōhoku Mountains}} {{Mountain-stub|pref=Akita Prefecture|pref2=Yamagata Prefecture}} [[Category:Mountain ranges in Japan]] [[Category:Mountains of Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Mountains of Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:Scenic spots in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Scenic spots in Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinjō City]] [[Category:Geography of Yuzawa City]] [[Category:Kaneyama, Yamagata]] [[Category:Mountains of the Ōu Range]] [[Category:1000-meter mountains]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:Kamuro-yama]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:Kamuro-yama]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[ja:神室山]] [[Category:Omonoimi faith]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] {{Omonoimi Faith}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox mountain | Name = Kamuro-yama (神室山) | Image = [[File:Kamuroyama.jpg|300px]] | Caption = Kamuro-yama and an emergency shelter (August 2011) | Elevation = 1365.2m | Coordinates = {{Coord|38|54|8|N|140|29|32.3|E|type:mountain_region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Location = {{flag|Japan}}
{{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県}} (Yuzawa City), {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}} (Shinjō City, Kaneyama Town) | Range = Kamuro Range | Type = Fault-block mountain ([[Granite]]) | First Ascent = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|38.902230|140.492306}}|caption=|width=300}}Location of Kamuro-yama }} '''Kamuro-yama''' (神室山) is a mountain that straddles the borders of {{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県}} (Yuzawa City) and {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}} (Shinjō City and Kaneyama Town), serving as the main peak of the Kamuro Range. It is one of the '''{{ill|200 Famous Japanese Mountains|ja|日本二百名山}}'''. This article also describes the Kamuro Range (Kamuro Mountain Range) in general. == Overview == The Kamuro Range, with Kamuro-yama as its main peak, stretches for over 30 km, comprising peaks such as {{ill|Kuro-mori|ja|黒森}} (1,057 m), {{ill|Suishō-mori|ja|水晶森}} (1,097 m), {{ill|Mae-Kamuro-yama|ja|前神室山}} (1,342 m), Kamuro-yama (1,365 m), {{ill|Tengu-mori|ja|天狗森}} (1,302 m), {{ill|Komata-yama|ja|小又山}} (1,366 m), {{ill|Hiuchi-dake|ja|火打岳}} (1,237 m), {{ill|Hachihori-yama|ja|八森山}} (1,098 m), {{ill|Mokuzō-yama|ja|杢蔵山}} (1,026 m), and {{ill|Ōmori-yama|ja|大森山}} (591 m). Although not exceptionally tall, its rugged appearance, sculpted by heavy snowfall, has earned it the nickname "The Mini-Alps of Tōhoku." The entire range is part of the {{ill|Kurikoma Quasi-National Park|ja|栗駒国定公園}}. Kamuro-yama has been a site of {{ill|mountain worship|ja|山岳信仰}} since ancient times, comparable to {{ill|Mount Chōkai|ja|鳥海山}} as a sacred mountain for ascetic practices. According to legend, it was opened by {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂}} during his eastern expeditions. The mountain has strong ties to Mount Chōkai, sharing the same deity, {{ill|Ōmonoimi no Kami|ja|大物忌神}}. It was revered as a deity of water and agriculture, with people climbing the mountain to pray for abundant harvests. Stone markers inscribed with names like "Kamuro Gongen," "Raijin," "Ōta-no-Kami," and "Suijin" remain on the summit, along with several sites thought to have been used for rituals, such as scattering rice to pray for bountiful harvests. Legends like "The Tengu of Kamuro-yama" also abound. Traces of pilgrim accommodations remain in the surrounding villages, such as Yamazaki Warabō in Kaneyama Town. Temples in the region also reflect the mountain's significance in {{ill|Shugendō|ja|修験道}}. Although Shugendō practices at Kamuro-yama declined due to concerns over moral corruption among monks, the mountain remains a site of local reverence. Despite its moderate elevation, heavy snowfall allows alpine plants to thrive at lower altitudes. Well-maintained trails make it a popular destination for hikers. == Hiking Information == * The emergency shelter at the summit was closed due to deterioration but was rebuilt over two years starting in 2009. It reopened in October 2010. A water source is located near the shelter. * Other water sources are limited to a few locations: near the Kamuro-yama shelter, Mokuzō Mountain Lodge, and the trail junction at "Sariguchi" between Komata-yama and Hiuchi-dake. * The ridges are narrow, posing a high risk of slipping. * The annual opening of the Kamuro Range in Shinjō City takes place in early June. [[File:Kamurosan Santyô.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Summit of Kamuro-yama (August 2011)]] == Access == === Akita Prefecture (Yuzawa City) === * '''Yakunai Trailhead''' ** '''Nishinokuchi Route''': This route follows Nishinokuchi River to the summit, featuring stone statues of Fudō Myōō, steep climbs like "Munazuka Hatchō," and scenic wetlands at Onta-no-Kami. ** '''Panorama Route''': This route passes Mae-Kamuro-yama and joins the Araya Route, featuring Kabutayama Shrine and scenic viewpoints. === Yamagata Prefecture (Kaneyama Town) === * '''Suishō-mori Trailhead''': Once a major route through the {{ill|Dewa Province|ja|出羽国}}, this trail leads to Suishō-mori and Mae-Kamuro-yama. * '''Araya Trailhead''': A route along Ōtaki Gorge leading to the summit. === Yamagata Prefecture (Shinjō City) === * '''Donai Trailhead''': This route joins the Kamazawa Trail and ascends via the Daiyama Ridge. * '''Sariguchi Trailhead''': A path through Donai Gorge to Sariguchi. * '''Hiuchi Shinmichi Trailhead''': Directly ascends Hiuchi-dake. * '''Hagino Trailhead''': A route to Hachihori-yama via Ippaimori. * '''San’ya Trailhead''': Passes through Mokuzō-yama and connects to other ridge trails. === Yamagata Prefecture (Mogami Town) === * '''Yakushihara Trailhead''': Ascends to Hachihori-yama. * '''Oyagurumi Trailhead''': Leads to the ridge between Komata-yama and Hachihori-yama. * '''Nishinokuchi Trailhead''': Starts near Shirakawa Dam and ascends Komata-yama. == Gallery == 三十三尋の滝２.jpg|Sanju-sanjihiro Falls on the Nishinokuchi Route 胸突八丁坂２.jpg|Munazuka Hatchō slope on the Nishinokuchi Route クマのひるね坂から御田の神.jpg|Onta-no-Kami seen from Kuma no Hirune slope 前神室山と雄勝・秋ノ宮の集落２.jpg|Mae-Kamuro-yama with Ogatsu and Akinomiya settlements 神室山山頂２.jpg|Summit of Kamuro-yama 避難小屋と雲海の広がる新庄盆地２.jpg|Shelter and sea of clouds over the Shinjō Basin (Gassan in the background) 栗駒山と虎毛山２.jpg|{{ill|Mount Kurikoma|ja|栗駒山}} (left) and {{ill|Torage-yama|ja|虎毛山}} (right) 前神室山.jpg|Mae-Kamuro-yama == Supplementary Notes == * Nearby is another mountain called {{ill|Karekake-dake|ja|禿岳}}, also pronounced "Kamuro-san." According to local lore, the two are considered sisters. * Surrounding sacred mountains include Kaneyama's {{ill|Ryūma-yama|ja|竜馬山}}, Mogami's Gongen-yama, Shinjō's Hachihori-yama, and the "Rokushō-dake" group in the {{ill|Kammu Prefectural Nature Park|ja|加無山県立自然公園}}. == External Links == * [https://maps.gsi.go.jp/#15/38.902230/140.492306/&base=std&ls=std&disp=1&vs=c1j0h0k0l0u0t0z0r0s0m0f1 Topographic Map of Kamuro-yama (Geospatial Information Authority of Japan)] * [https://www.city.shinjo.yamagata.jp/k001/020/050/150/20150313172806.html Kamuro-yama Shelter Information (Shinjō City Official Website)] * [http://www.ecopolis.or.jp/kanko/ Mogami Regional Tourism Council] {{200 Famous Japanese Mountains}} {{100 Famous Tōhoku Mountains}} {{Mountain-stub|pref=Akita Prefecture|pref2=Yamagata Prefecture}} [[Category:Mountain ranges in Japan]] [[Category:Mountains of Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Mountains of Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:Scenic spots in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Scenic spots in Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinjō City]] [[Category:Geography of Yuzawa City]] [[Category:Kaneyama, Yamagata]] [[Category:Mountains of the Ōu Range]] [[Category:1000-meter mountains]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Kamuro-yama]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kamuro-yama]][[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[ja:神室山]] [[Category:Omonoimi faith]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] {{Omonoimi Faith}}

revision.comment: /* 山の概要 */ 別名に「みちのくのアルプス」を追記、出典を添付。
revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2023年8月}} {{Infobox 山 |名称 = 神室山 |画像 = [[File:Kamuroyama.jpg|300px]] |画像キャプション = 神室山と避難小屋 (2011年8月) |標高 = 1365.2 |座標 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|38|54|8|N|140|29|32.3|E|type:mountain_region:JP|display=inline,title}} |所在地 = {{JPN}}
[[秋田県]][[湯沢市]]・
[[山形県]][[新庄市]]・[[金山町 (山形県)|金山町]] |山系 = 神室連峰 |種類 = 構造山地（[[花崗岩]]） |初登頂 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|38.902230|140.492306}}|caption=|width=300}}神室山の位置 }} '''神室山'''（かむろさん）は[[秋田県]][[湯沢市]]と[[山形県]][[新庄市]]、[[金山町 (山形県)|金山町]]の県境･市境上にそびえる、神室連峰の主峰となる山である。 『'''[[日本二百名山]]'''』のひとつに数えられる。 この項では、便宜的に神室連峰（神室山地）全般についても記述する。 == 山の概要 == 神室山を主峰とする神室連峰は、黒森(1,057m)から、水晶森(1,097m)、前神室山(1,342m)、神室山(1,365m)、天狗森(1,302m)、小又山(1,366m)、火打岳(1,237m)、八森山(1,098m)、[[杢蔵山]](1,026m)、大森山(591m)と、脊梁が30kmあまりも続く[[山脈]]であり、標高こそ高くはないものの豪雪に磨かれた山容は険しく、そのことから別名「'''東北のミニ[[アルプス山脈|アルプス]]'''」「'''みちのくのアルプス'''」と呼ばれる{{Cite web |title=【募集終了】東北のミニアルプス『神室山』を満喫できる農泊体験 |url=https://yamagatayama.com/news/cat_03/%E3%80%90%E5%8F%82%E5%8A%A0%E8%80%85%E5%8B%9F%E9%9B%86%E3%80%91%E6%9D%B1%E5%8C%97%E3%81%AE%E3%83%9F%E3%83%8B%E3%82%A2%E3%83%AB%E3%83%97%E3%82%B9%E3%80%8E%E7%A5%9E%E5%AE%A4%E5%B1%B1%E3%80%8F%E3%82%92/ |website=やまがた山（山形県山岳情報ポータルサイト） |access-date=2025-02-18 |language=ja}}{{Cite web |url=https://www.pref.yamagata.jp/documents/4081/tourism_kamuro.pdf |title=神室ダム［ダム湖とブナ原生林が織り成す自然景観］ |access-date=2025年2月19日 |publisher=[[山形県]]}}{{Cite web |title=神室山 |url=https://yamagatayama.com/hyakumeizan/no-028/ |website=やまがた山（山形県山岳情報ポータルサイト） |access-date=2025-02-18 |language=ja}}{{Cite web |title=神室山 |url=https://kanko-mogami.jp/spot/mt-kamuro/ |website=【公式】最上地域の観光情報サイト - AMAZINGMOGAMI |access-date=2025-02-18 |language=ja}}。全山[[栗駒国定公園]]に指定されている。 神室山は、古来から[[山岳信仰]]の山として知られ、修験の山として[[鳥海山]]と並び称される存在であった。伝説によれば、[[坂上田村麻呂]]が東征のおりに開山したと伝えられている。鳥海山との結びつきが強く、祭神も同じ[[大物忌神]]である。また、神室山は水や[[農業]]の神として敬われ、人々は山に登っては[[五穀]]豊穣を祈っていた。山頂には「神室権現」、「雷神」、「太田神」、「水神」といった石碑が残されており、山中の窪地を[[田|田んぼ]]に見立て、米などを撒いて五穀豊穣を祈った「御田」の跡と推定される場所が何箇所か残っている。また、「神室山の[[天狗]]」など多くの伝承が言い伝えられている。神室山麓の各集落には、それぞれに神室山の参詣者を迎えた[[宿坊]]の跡とされる場所が残されている。例えば、金山町の山崎藁坊はその一つである。また、金山町内に残る[[寺]]は、神室山の山岳修験にちなんでいる。神室山の[[修験道]]自体は、僧坊に女を囲うなど、破戒行為が著しいことを理由に禁止され、廃れて行ったが、近隣の住民の信仰の対象とされ続けている。 他の山脈に比べて特別標高が高いわけではないが、積雪の多さから比較的低標高に[[高山植物]]が群生しており、[[登山道]]も比較的整備されているため、人気のある登山コースとなっている。 == 登山情報 == * 山頂にあった[[山小屋|避難小屋]]は、老朽化のため使用禁止になっていた。[[2008年]]9月、利用者の要望が強まったことにより、[[2009年]]度から2か年かけて、避難小屋の全面改築を行うことを決めた。[http://yamagata-np.jp/news/200809/30/kj_2008093000474.php]2010年10月に避難小屋は完成し、利用できるようになっている。避難小屋の近くには水場がある。 * 水場は、縦走ルート上には、神室山避難小屋と杢蔵山荘、小又山と火打岳の間にある登山道の分岐点、「砂利口」から少し下った場所にしかない。 * 尾根は痩せているので、滑落の危険性が高い。 * 新庄市の神室連峰山開きは、毎年6月上旬の週末に行われる。 [[ファイル:Kamurosan Santyô.jpg|thumb|250px|right|神室山山頂（2011年8月）]] == アクセス == * 秋田県湯沢市 ** '''役内口'''　 *** 西の又ルート（西の又川に沿って神室山頂上を目指すルート。途中に不動明王の石仏がある最後の水場、胸突八丁の急登、池塘やお花畑の御田ノ神（おんたのかみ）がある） *** パノラマルート（前神室山を通り、有屋口からのルートと合流後、国定公園のレリーフと石像がある鏑山神社（レリーフピーク）を通過するルート） * 山形県金山町 ** '''水晶森口'''（かつて出羽を縦貫するメインルートであった[[有屋峠]]のルート。稜線上に出て、水晶森～前神室山を経由する） ** '''有屋口'''（大滝沿いの登山道から神室山頂上を目指す。山形県側からの代表的な登山ルート） ** '''蒲沢口'''（権八小屋跡で土内口からの登山道と合流し、台山尾根から神室山頂上を目指すルート） * 山形県新庄市 ** '''土内口'''（権八小屋跡で蒲沢口からの登山道と合流し、台山尾根から神室山頂上を目指すルート） ** '''砂利押沢口'''（土内から土内渓谷を通って、砂利口に出るルート。途中に水場がある） ** '''火打新道口'''（土内から火打岳を直接登るルート） ** '''萩野口'''（萩野から一杯森を通って、八森山を目指すルート） ** '''山屋口'''（[[杢蔵山]]を経由し、尾根を縦走するルート。登山道入口に新庄市営山屋キャンプ場、前杢蔵に[[山小屋]]「杢蔵山荘」あり） * 山形県[[最上町]] ** '''薬師原口'''（薬師原から八森山を目指すルート） ** '''親倉見口'''（親倉見から八森山と小又山との間にある稜線、槍が先を目指すルート） ** '''西の又口'''（白川ダムから延びる西の又林道の終点から、小又山を目指すルート） == ギャラリー == 三十三尋の滝２.jpg|西ノ又コースの三十三尋の滝 胸突八丁坂２.jpg|西ノ又コースの胸突八丁坂 クマのひるね坂から御田の神.jpg|クマのひるね坂から御田の神 前神室山と雄勝・秋ノ宮の集落２.jpg|前神室山と雄勝・秋ノ宮の集落 神室山山頂２.jpg|神室山山頂 避難小屋と雲海の広がる新庄盆地２.jpg|避難小屋と[[雲海]]広がる[[新庄盆地]](左奥は[[月山]]) 栗駒山と虎毛山２.jpg|[[栗駒山]]（左奥）と[[虎毛山]]（右手前） 前神室山.jpg|前神室山 == 補足 == * 神室山の近くにはもう一つ、[[奥羽山脈]]に属する[[禿岳]]（小鏑山）がありこちらも「かむろだけ」「かむろさん」と呼ばれている。同じ呼び名であることから、両山は姉妹であるとされており、山麓では姉妹神の争いの伝承が伝えられている。禿岳を区別のため「はげかむろ」と呼ぶ人もいる。 * 前述の通り、神室山は山岳信仰の山であったが、その周囲にも山岳信仰の山は多い。金山町の「[[竜馬山_(山形県)|竜馬山]]」、最上町の「権現山」、神室連峰の1峰でもある新庄市の八森山、「甑岳」と総称される[[加無山県立自然公園]]などである。 == 外部リンク == * [https://maps.gsi.go.jp/#15/38.902230/140.492306/&base=std&ls=std&disp=1&vs=c1j0h0k0l0u0t0z0r0s0m0f1 地図閲覧サービス 2万5千分1地形図名：神室山 北東] * [https://www.city.shinjo.yamagata.jp/k001/020/050/150/20150313172806.html 神室山避難小屋の利用について(新庄市ホームページ)] * [http://www.ecopolis.or.jp/kanko/ 最上地域観光協議会] {{日本二百名山}} {{東北百名山}} {{Mountain-stub|pref=秋田県|pref2=山形県}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かむろさん}} [[Category:山岳名目録]] [[Category:山形県の山]] [[Category:秋田県の山]] [[Category:山形県の自然景勝地]] [[Category:秋田県の自然景勝地]] [[Category:新庄市]] [[Category:湯沢市の地理]] [[Category:金山町 (山形県)]] [[Category:奥羽山脈の山]] [[Category:1000メートル峰]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox mountain | Name = Kamuro-yama (神室山) | Image = [[File:Kamuroyama.jpg|300px]] | Caption = Kamuro-yama and an emergency shelter (August 2011) | Elevation = 1365.2m | Coordinates = {{Coord|38|54|8|N|140|29|32.3|E|type:mountain_region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Location = {{flag|Japan}}
{{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県}} (Yuzawa City), {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}} (Shinjō City, Kaneyama Town) | Range = Kamuro Range | Type = Fault-block mountain ([[Granite]]) | First Ascent = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|38.902230|140.492306}}|caption=|width=300}}Location of Kamuro-yama }} '''Kamuro-yama''' (神室山) is a mountain that straddles the borders of {{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県}} (Yuzawa City) and {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}} (Shinjō City and Kaneyama Town), serving as the main peak of the Kamuro Range. It is one of the '''{{ill|200 Famous Japanese Mountains|ja|日本二百名山}}'''. This article also describes the Kamuro Range (Kamuro Mountain Range) in general. == Overview == The Kamuro Range, with Kamuro-yama as its main peak, stretches for over 30 km, comprising peaks such as {{ill|Kuro-mori|ja|黒森}} (1,057 m), {{ill|Suishō-mori|ja|水晶森}} (1,097 m), {{ill|Mae-Kamuro-yama|ja|前神室山}} (1,342 m), Kamuro-yama (1,365 m), {{ill|Tengu-mori|ja|天狗森}} (1,302 m), {{ill|Komata-yama|ja|小又山}} (1,366 m), {{ill|Hiuchi-dake|ja|火打岳}} (1,237 m), {{ill|Hachihori-yama|ja|八森山}} (1,098 m), {{ill|Mokuzō-yama|ja|杢蔵山}} (1,026 m), and {{ill|Ōmori-yama|ja|大森山}} (591 m). Although not exceptionally tall, its rugged appearance, sculpted by heavy snowfall, has earned it the nickname "The Mini-Alps of Tōhoku." The entire range is part of the {{ill|Kurikoma Quasi-National Park|ja|栗駒国定公園}}. Kamuro-yama has been a site of {{ill|mountain worship|ja|山岳信仰}} since ancient times, comparable to {{ill|Mount Chōkai|ja|鳥海山}} as a sacred mountain for ascetic practices. According to legend, it was opened by {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂}} during his eastern expeditions. The mountain has strong ties to Mount Chōkai, sharing the same deity, {{ill|Ōmonoimi no Kami|ja|大物忌神}}. It was revered as a deity of water and agriculture, with people climbing the mountain to pray for abundant harvests. Stone markers inscribed with names like "Kamuro Gongen," "Raijin," "Ōta-no-Kami," and "Suijin" remain on the summit, along with several sites thought to have been used for rituals, such as scattering rice to pray for bountiful harvests. Legends like "The Tengu of Kamuro-yama" also abound. Traces of pilgrim accommodations remain in the surrounding villages, such as Yamazaki Warabō in Kaneyama Town. Temples in the region also reflect the mountain's significance in {{ill|Shugendō|ja|修験道}}. Although Shugendō practices at Kamuro-yama declined due to concerns over moral corruption among monks, the mountain remains a site of local reverence. Despite its moderate elevation, heavy snowfall allows alpine plants to thrive at lower altitudes. Well-maintained trails make it a popular destination for hikers. == Hiking Information == * The emergency shelter at the summit was closed due to deterioration but was rebuilt over two years starting in 2009. It reopened in October 2010. A water source is located near the shelter. * Other water sources are limited to a few locations: near the Kamuro-yama shelter, Mokuzō Mountain Lodge, and the trail junction at "Sariguchi" between Komata-yama and Hiuchi-dake. * The ridges are narrow, posing a high risk of slipping. * The annual opening of the Kamuro Range in Shinjō City takes place in early June. [[File:Kamurosan Santyô.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Summit of Kamuro-yama (August 2011)]] == Access == === Akita Prefecture (Yuzawa City) === * '''Yakunai Trailhead''' ** '''Nishinokuchi Route''': This route follows Nishinokuchi River to the summit, featuring stone statues of Fudō Myōō, steep climbs like "Munazuka Hatchō," and scenic wetlands at Onta-no-Kami. ** '''Panorama Route''': This route passes Mae-Kamuro-yama and joins the Araya Route, featuring Kabutayama Shrine and scenic viewpoints. === Yamagata Prefecture (Kaneyama Town) === * '''Suishō-mori Trailhead''': Once a major route through the {{ill|Dewa Province|ja|出羽国}}, this trail leads to Suishō-mori and Mae-Kamuro-yama. * '''Araya Trailhead''': A route along Ōtaki Gorge leading to the summit. === Yamagata Prefecture (Shinjō City) === * '''Donai Trailhead''': This route joins the Kamazawa Trail and ascends via the Daiyama Ridge. * '''Sariguchi Trailhead''': A path through Donai Gorge to Sariguchi. * '''Hiuchi Shinmichi Trailhead''': Directly ascends Hiuchi-dake. * '''Hagino Trailhead''': A route to Hachihori-yama via Ippaimori. * '''San’ya Trailhead''': Passes through Mokuzō-yama and connects to other ridge trails. === Yamagata Prefecture (Mogami Town) === * '''Yakushihara Trailhead''': Ascends to Hachihori-yama. * '''Oyagurumi Trailhead''': Leads to the ridge between Komata-yama and Hachihori-yama. * '''Nishinokuchi Trailhead''': Starts near Shirakawa Dam and ascends Komata-yama. == Gallery == 三十三尋の滝２.jpg|Sanju-sanjihiro Falls on the Nishinokuchi Route 胸突八丁坂２.jpg|Munazuka Hatchō slope on the Nishinokuchi Route クマのひるね坂から御田の神.jpg|Onta-no-Kami seen from Kuma no Hirune slope 前神室山と雄勝・秋ノ宮の集落２.jpg|Mae-Kamuro-yama with Ogatsu and Akinomiya settlements 神室山山頂２.jpg|Summit of Kamuro-yama 避難小屋と雲海の広がる新庄盆地２.jpg|Shelter and sea of clouds over the Shinjō Basin (Gassan in the background) 栗駒山と虎毛山２.jpg|{{ill|Mount Kurikoma|ja|栗駒山}} (left) and {{ill|Torage-yama|ja|虎毛山}} (right) 前神室山.jpg|Mae-Kamuro-yama == Supplementary Notes == * Nearby is another mountain called {{ill|Karekake-dake|ja|禿岳}}, also pronounced "Kamuro-san." According to local lore, the two are considered sisters. * Surrounding sacred mountains include Kaneyama's {{ill|Ryūma-yama|ja|竜馬山}}, Mogami's Gongen-yama, Shinjō's Hachihori-yama, and the "Rokushō-dake" group in the {{ill|Kammu Prefectural Nature Park|ja|加無山県立自然公園}}. == External Links == * [https://maps.gsi.go.jp/#15/38.902230/140.492306/&base=std&ls=std&disp=1&vs=c1j0h0k0l0u0t0z0r0s0m0f1 Topographic Map of Kamuro-yama (Geospatial Information Authority of Japan)] * [https://www.city.shinjo.yamagata.jp/k001/020/050/150/20150313172806.html Kamuro-yama Shelter Information (Shinjō City Official Website)] * [http://www.ecopolis.or.jp/kanko/ Mogami Regional Tourism Council] {{200 Famous Japanese Mountains}} {{100 Famous Tōhoku Mountains}} {{Mountain-stub|pref=Akita Prefecture|pref2=Yamagata Prefecture}} [[Category:Mountain ranges in Japan]] [[Category:Mountains of Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Mountains of Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:Scenic spots in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Scenic spots in Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinjō City]] [[Category:Geography of Yuzawa City]] [[Category:Kaneyama, Yamagata]] [[Category:Mountains of the Ōu Range]] [[Category:1000-meter mountains]] [[Category:Kamuro-yama]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Kamuro-yama-qq]] [[Category:Kamuro-yama]][[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[ja:神室山]] [[Category:Omonoimi faith]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] {{Omonoimi Faith}}

According to local lore, the two are considered sisters. * Surrounding sacred mountains include Kaneyama's {{ill|Ryūma-yama|ja|竜馬山}}, Mogami's Gongen-yama, Shinjō's Hachihori-yama, and the "Rokushō-dake" group in the {{ill|Kammu Prefectural Nature Park|ja|加無山県立自然公園}}. == External Links == * [https://maps.gsi.go.jp/#15/38.902230/140.492306/&base=std&ls=std&disp=1&vs=c1j0h0k0l0u0t0z0r0s0m0f1 Topographic Map of Kamuro-yama (Geospatial Information Authority of Japan)] * [https://www.city.shinjo.yamagata.jp/k001/020/050/150/20150313172806.html Kamuro-yama Shelter Information (Shinjō City Official Website)] * [http://www.ecopolis.or.jp/kanko/ Mogami Regional Tourism Council] {{200 Famous Japanese Mountains}} {{100 Famous Tōhoku Mountains}} {{Mountain-stub|pref=Akita Prefecture|pref2=Yamagata Prefecture}} [[Category:Mountain ranges in Japan]] [[Category:Mountains of Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Mountains of Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:Scenic spots in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Scenic spots in Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinjō City]] [[Category:Geography of Yuzawa City]] [[Category:Kaneyama, Yamagata]] [[Category:Mountains of the Ōu Range]] [[Category:1000-meter mountains]] [[Category:Kamuro-yama]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Kamuro-yama-qq]] [[Category:Kamuro-yama]][[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[ja:神室山]] [[Category:Omonoimi faith]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] {{Omonoimi Faith}}

{{Infobox mountain | Name = Kamuro-yama (神室山) | Image = [[File:Kamuroyama.jpg|300px]] | Caption = Kamuro-yama and an emergency shelter (August 2011) | Elevation = 1365.2m | Coordinates = {{Coord|38|54|8|N|140|29|32.3|E|type:mountain_region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Location = {{flag|Japan}}
{{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県}} (Yuzawa City), {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}} (Shinjō City, Kaneyama Town) | Range = Kamuro Range | Type = Fault-block mountain ([[Granite]]) | First Ascent = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|38.902230|140.492306}}|caption=|width=300}}Location of Kamuro-yama }} '''Kamuro-yama''' (神室山) is a mountain that straddles the borders of {{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県}} (Yuzawa City) and {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}} (Shinjō City and Kaneyama Town), serving as the main peak of the Kamuro Range. It is one of the '''{{ill|200 Famous Japanese Mountains|ja|日本二百名山}}'''. This article also describes the Kamuro Range (Kamuro Mountain Range) in general. == Overview == The Kamuro Range, with Kamuro-yama as its main peak, stretches for over 30 km, comprising peaks such as {{ill|Kuro-mori|ja|黒森}} (1,057 m), {{ill|Suishō-mori|ja|水晶森}} (1,097 m), {{ill|Mae-Kamuro-yama|ja|前神室山}} (1,342 m), Kamuro-yama (1,365 m), {{ill|Tengu-mori|ja|天狗森}} (1,302 m), {{ill|Komata-yama|ja|小又山}} (1,366 m), {{ill|Hiuchi-dake|ja|火打岳}} (1,237 m), {{ill|Hachihori-yama|ja|八森山}} (1,098 m), {{ill|Mokuzō-yama|ja|杢蔵山}} (1,026 m), and {{ill|Ōmori-yama|ja|大森山}} (591 m). Although not exceptionally tall, its rugged appearance, sculpted by heavy snowfall, has earned it the nickname "The Mini-Alps of Tōhoku." The entire range is part of the {{ill|Kurikoma Quasi-National Park|ja|栗駒国定公園}}. Kamuro-yama has been a site of {{ill|mountain worship|ja|山岳信仰}} since ancient times, comparable to {{ill|Mount Chōkai|ja|鳥海山}} as a sacred mountain for ascetic practices. According to legend, it was opened by {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂}} during his eastern expeditions. The mountain has strong ties to Mount Chōkai, sharing the same deity, {{ill|Ōmonoimi no Kami|ja|大物忌神}}. It was revered as a deity of water and agriculture, with people climbing the mountain to pray for abundant harvests. Stone markers inscribed with names like "Kamuro Gongen," "Raijin," "Ōta-no-Kami," and "Suijin" remain on the summit, along with several sites thought to have been used for rituals, such as scattering rice to pray for bountiful harvests. Legends like "The Tengu of Kamuro-yama" also abound. Traces of pilgrim accommodations remain in the surrounding villages, such as Yamazaki Warabō in Kaneyama Town. Temples in the region also reflect the mountain's significance in {{ill|Shugendō|ja|修験道}}. Although Shugendō practices at Kamuro-yama declined due to concerns over moral corruption among monks, the mountain remains a site of local reverence. Despite its moderate elevation, heavy snowfall allows alpine plants to thrive at lower altitudes. Well-maintained trails make it a popular destination for hikers. == Hiking Information == * The emergency shelter at the summit was closed due to deterioration but was rebuilt over two years starting in 2009. It reopened in October 2010. A water source is located near the shelter. * Other water sources are limited to a few locations: near the Kamuro-yama shelter, Mokuzō Mountain Lodge, and the trail junction at "Sariguchi" between Komata-yama and Hiuchi-dake. * The ridges are narrow, posing a high risk of slipping. * The annual opening of the Kamuro Range in Shinjō City takes place in early June. [[File:Kamurosan Santyô.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Summit of Kamuro-yama (August 2011)]] == Access == === Akita Prefecture (Yuzawa City) === * '''Yakunai Trailhead''' ** '''Nishinokuchi Route''': This route follows Nishinokuchi River to the summit, featuring stone statues of Fudō Myōō, steep climbs like "Munazuka Hatchō," and scenic wetlands at Onta-no-Kami. ** '''Panorama Route''': This route passes Mae-Kamuro-yama and joins the Araya Route, featuring Kabutayama Shrine and scenic viewpoints. === Yamagata Prefecture (Kaneyama Town) === * '''Suishō-mori Trailhead''': Once a major route through the {{ill|Dewa Province|ja|出羽国}}, this trail leads to Suishō-mori and Mae-Kamuro-yama. * '''Araya Trailhead''': A route along Ōtaki Gorge leading to the summit. === Yamagata Prefecture (Shinjō City) === * '''Donai Trailhead''': This route joins the Kamazawa Trail and ascends via the Daiyama Ridge. * '''Sariguchi Trailhead''': A path through Donai Gorge to Sariguchi. * '''Hiuchi Shinmichi Trailhead''': Directly ascends Hiuchi-dake. * '''Hagino Trailhead''': A route to Hachihori-yama via Ippaimori. * '''San’ya Trailhead''': Passes through Mokuzō-yama and connects to other ridge trails. === Yamagata Prefecture (Mogami Town) === * '''Yakushihara Trailhead''': Ascends to Hachihori-yama. * '''Oyagurumi Trailhead''': Leads to the ridge between Komata-yama and Hachihori-yama. * '''Nishinokuchi Trailhead''': Starts near Shirakawa Dam and ascends Komata-yama. == Gallery == 三十三尋の滝２.jpg|Sanju-sanjihiro Falls on the Nishinokuchi Route 胸突八丁坂２.jpg|Munazuka Hatchō slope on the Nishinokuchi Route クマのひるね坂から御田の神.jpg|Onta-no-Kami seen from Kuma no Hirune slope 前神室山と雄勝・秋ノ宮の集落２.jpg|Mae-Kamuro-yama with Ogatsu and Akinomiya settlements 神室山山頂２.jpg|Summit of Kamuro-yama 避難小屋と雲海の広がる新庄盆地２.jpg|Shelter and sea of clouds over the Shinjō Basin (Gassan in the background) 栗駒山と虎毛山２.jpg|{{ill|Mount Kurikoma|ja|栗駒山}} (left) and {{ill|Torage-yama|ja|虎毛山}} (right) 前神室山.jpg|Mae-Kamuro-yama == Supplementary Notes == * Nearby is another mountain called {{ill|Karekake-dake|ja|禿岳}}, also pronounced "Kamuro-san." According to local lore, the two are considered sisters. * Surrounding sacred mountains include Kaneyama's {{ill|Ryūma-yama|ja|竜馬山}}, Mogami's Gongen-yama, Shinjō's Hachihori-yama, and the "Rokushō-dake" group in the {{ill|Kammu Prefectural Nature Park|ja|加無山県立自然公園}}. == External Links == * [https://maps.gsi.go.jp/#15/38.902230/140.492306/&base=std&ls=std&disp=1&vs=c1j0h0k0l0u0t0z0r0s0m0f1 Topographic Map of Kamuro-yama (Geospatial Information Authority of Japan)] * [https://www.city.shinjo.yamagata.jp/k001/020/050/150/20150313172806.html Kamuro-yama Shelter Information (Shinjō City Official Website)] * [http://www.ecopolis.or.jp/kanko/ Mogami Regional Tourism Council] {{200 Famous Japanese Mountains}} {{100 Famous Tōhoku Mountains}} {{Mountain-stub|pref=Akita Prefecture|pref2=Yamagata Prefecture}} [[Category:Mountain ranges in Japan]] [[Category:Mountains of Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Mountains of Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:Scenic spots in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Scenic spots in Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinjō City]] [[Category:Geography of Yuzawa City]] [[Category:Kaneyama, Yamagata]] [[Category:Mountains of the Ōu Range]] [[Category:1000-meter mountains]] [[Category:Kamuro-yama]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kamuro-yama-qq]] [[Category:Kamuro-yama]][[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[ja:神室山]] [[Category:Omonoimi faith]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] {{Omonoimi Faith}}

According to local lore, the two are considered sisters. * Surrounding sacred mountains include Kaneyama's {{ill|Ryūma-yama|ja|竜馬山}}, Mogami's Gongen-yama, Shinjō's Hachihori-yama, and the "Rokushō-dake" group in the {{ill|Kammu Prefectural Nature Park|ja|加無山県立自然公園}}. == External Links == * [https://maps.gsi.go.jp/#15/38.902230/140.492306/&base=std&ls=std&disp=1&vs=c1j0h0k0l0u0t0z0r0s0m0f1 Topographic Map of Kamuro-yama (Geospatial Information Authority of Japan)] * [https://www.city.shinjo.yamagata.jp/k001/020/050/150/20150313172806.html Kamuro-yama Shelter Information (Shinjō City Official Website)] * [http://www.ecopolis.or.jp/kanko/ Mogami Regional Tourism Council] {{200 Famous Japanese Mountains}} {{100 Famous Tōhoku Mountains}} {{Mountain-stub|pref=Akita Prefecture|pref2=Yamagata Prefecture}} [[Category:Mountain ranges in Japan]] [[Category:Mountains of Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Mountains of Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:Scenic spots in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Scenic spots in Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinjō City]] [[Category:Geography of Yuzawa City]] [[Category:Kaneyama, Yamagata]] [[Category:Mountains of the Ōu Range]] [[Category:1000-meter mountains]] [[Category:Kamuro-yama]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kamuro-yama-qq]] [[Category:Kamuro-yama]][[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[ja:神室山]] [[Category:Omonoimi faith]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] {{Omonoimi Faith}}

{{Infobox mountain | Name = Kamuro-yama (神室山) | Image = [[File:Kamuroyama.jpg|300px]] | Caption = Kamuro-yama and an emergency shelter (August 2011) | Elevation = 1365.2m | Coordinates = {{Coord|38|54|8|N|140|29|32.3|E|type:mountain_region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Location = {{flag|Japan}}
{{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県}} (Yuzawa City), {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}} (Shinjō City, Kaneyama Town) | Range = Kamuro Range | Type = Fault-block mountain ([[Granite]]) | First Ascent = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|38.902230|140.492306}}|caption=|width=300}}Location of Kamuro-yama }} '''Kamuro-yama''' (神室山) is a mountain that straddles the borders of {{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県}} (Yuzawa City) and {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}} (Shinjō City and Kaneyama Town), serving as the main peak of the Kamuro Range. It is one of the '''{{ill|200 Famous Japanese Mountains|ja|日本二百名山}}'''. This article also describes the Kamuro Range (Kamuro Mountain Range) in general. == Overview == The Kamuro Range, with Kamuro-yama as its main peak, stretches for over 30 km, comprising peaks such as {{ill|Kuro-mori|ja|黒森}} (1,057 m), {{ill|Suishō-mori|ja|水晶森}} (1,097 m), {{ill|Mae-Kamuro-yama|ja|前神室山}} (1,342 m), Kamuro-yama (1,365 m), {{ill|Tengu-mori|ja|天狗森}} (1,302 m), {{ill|Komata-yama|ja|小又山}} (1,366 m), {{ill|Hiuchi-dake|ja|火打岳}} (1,237 m), {{ill|Hachihori-yama|ja|八森山}} (1,098 m), {{ill|Mokuzō-yama|ja|杢蔵山}} (1,026 m), and {{ill|Ōmori-yama|ja|大森山}} (591 m). Although not exceptionally tall, its rugged appearance, sculpted by heavy snowfall, has earned it the nickname "The Mini-Alps of Tōhoku." The entire range is part of the {{ill|Kurikoma Quasi-National Park|ja|栗駒国定公園}}. Kamuro-yama has been a site of {{ill|mountain worship|ja|山岳信仰}} since ancient times, comparable to {{ill|Mount Chōkai|ja|鳥海山}} as a sacred mountain for ascetic practices. According to legend, it was opened by {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂}} during his eastern expeditions. The mountain has strong ties to Mount Chōkai, sharing the same deity, {{ill|Ōmonoimi no Kami|ja|大物忌神}}. It was revered as a deity of water and agriculture, with people climbing the mountain to pray for abundant harvests. Stone markers inscribed with names like "Kamuro Gongen," "Raijin," "Ōta-no-Kami," and "Suijin" remain on the summit, along with several sites thought to have been used for rituals, such as scattering rice to pray for bountiful harvests. Legends like "The Tengu of Kamuro-yama" also abound. Traces of pilgrim accommodations remain in the surrounding villages, such as Yamazaki Warabō in Kaneyama Town. Temples in the region also reflect the mountain's significance in {{ill|Shugendō|ja|修験道}}. Although Shugendō practices at Kamuro-yama declined due to concerns over moral corruption among monks, the mountain remains a site of local reverence. Despite its moderate elevation, heavy snowfall allows alpine plants to thrive at lower altitudes. Well-maintained trails make it a popular destination for hikers. == Hiking Information == * The emergency shelter at the summit was closed due to deterioration but was rebuilt over two years starting in 2009. It reopened in October 2010. A water source is located near the shelter. * Other water sources are limited to a few locations: near the Kamuro-yama shelter, Mokuzō Mountain Lodge, and the trail junction at "Sariguchi" between Komata-yama and Hiuchi-dake. * The ridges are narrow, posing a high risk of slipping. * The annual opening of the Kamuro Range in Shinjō City takes place in early June. [[File:Kamurosan Santyô.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Summit of Kamuro-yama (August 2011)]] == Access == === Akita Prefecture (Yuzawa City) === * '''Yakunai Trailhead''' ** '''Nishinokuchi Route''': This route follows Nishinokuchi River to the summit, featuring stone statues of Fudō Myōō, steep climbs like "Munazuka Hatchō," and scenic wetlands at Onta-no-Kami. ** '''Panorama Route''': This route passes Mae-Kamuro-yama and joins the Araya Route, featuring Kabutayama Shrine and scenic viewpoints. === Yamagata Prefecture (Kaneyama Town) === * '''Suishō-mori Trailhead''': Once a major route through the {{ill|Dewa Province|ja|出羽国}}, this trail leads to Suishō-mori and Mae-Kamuro-yama. * '''Araya Trailhead''': A route along Ōtaki Gorge leading to the summit. === Yamagata Prefecture (Shinjō City) === * '''Donai Trailhead''': This route joins the Kamazawa Trail and ascends via the Daiyama Ridge. * '''Sariguchi Trailhead''': A path through Donai Gorge to Sariguchi. * '''Hiuchi Shinmichi Trailhead''': Directly ascends Hiuchi-dake. * '''Hagino Trailhead''': A route to Hachihori-yama via Ippaimori. * '''San’ya Trailhead''': Passes through Mokuzō-yama and connects to other ridge trails. === Yamagata Prefecture (Mogami Town) === * '''Yakushihara Trailhead''': Ascends to Hachihori-yama. * '''Oyagurumi Trailhead''': Leads to the ridge between Komata-yama and Hachihori-yama. * '''Nishinokuchi Trailhead''': Starts near Shirakawa Dam and ascends Komata-yama. == Gallery == 三十三尋の滝２.jpg|Sanju-sanjihiro Falls on the Nishinokuchi Route 胸突八丁坂２.jpg|Munazuka Hatchō slope on the Nishinokuchi Route クマのひるね坂から御田の神.jpg|Onta-no-Kami seen from Kuma no Hirune slope 前神室山と雄勝・秋ノ宮の集落２.jpg|Mae-Kamuro-yama with Ogatsu and Akinomiya settlements 神室山山頂２.jpg|Summit of Kamuro-yama 避難小屋と雲海の広がる新庄盆地２.jpg|Shelter and sea of clouds over the Shinjō Basin (Gassan in the background) 栗駒山と虎毛山２.jpg|{{ill|Mount Kurikoma|ja|栗駒山}} (left) and {{ill|Torage-yama|ja|虎毛山}} (right) 前神室山.jpg|Mae-Kamuro-yama == Supplementary Notes == * Nearby is another mountain called {{ill|Karekake-dake|ja|禿岳}}, also pronounced "Kamuro-san." According to local lore, the two are considered sisters. * Surrounding sacred mountains include Kaneyama's {{ill|Ryūma-yama|ja|竜馬山}}, Mogami's Gongen-yama, Shinjō's Hachihori-yama, and the "Rokushō-dake" group in the {{ill|Kammu Prefectural Nature Park|ja|加無山県立自然公園}}. == External Links == * [https://maps.gsi.go.jp/#15/38.902230/140.492306/&base=std&ls=std&disp=1&vs=c1j0h0k0l0u0t0z0r0s0m0f1 Topographic Map of Kamuro-yama (Geospatial Information Authority of Japan)] * [https://www.city.shinjo.yamagata.jp/k001/020/050/150/20150313172806.html Kamuro-yama Shelter Information (Shinjō City Official Website)] * [http://www.ecopolis.or.jp/kanko/ Mogami Regional Tourism Council] {{200 Famous Japanese Mountains}} {{100 Famous Tōhoku Mountains}} {{Mountain-stub|pref=Akita Prefecture|pref2=Yamagata Prefecture}} [[Category:Mountain ranges in Japan]] [[Category:Mountains of Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Mountains of Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:Scenic spots in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Scenic spots in Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinjō City]] [[Category:Geography of Yuzawa City]] [[Category:Kaneyama, Yamagata]] [[Category:Mountains of the Ōu Range]] [[Category:1000-meter mountains]] [[Category:Kamuro-yama]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kamuro-yama-qq]] [[Category:Kamuro-yama]][[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[ja:神室山]] [[Category:Omonoimi faith]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] {{Omonoimi Faith}}

According to local lore, the two are considered sisters. * Surrounding sacred mountains include Kaneyama's {{ill|Ryūma-yama|ja|竜馬山}}, Mogami's Gongen-yama, Shinjō's Hachihori-yama, and the "Rokushō-dake" group in the {{ill|Kammu Prefectural Nature Park|ja|加無山県立自然公園}}. == External Links == * [https://maps.gsi.go.jp/#15/38.902230/140.492306/&base=std&ls=std&disp=1&vs=c1j0h0k0l0u0t0z0r0s0m0f1 Topographic Map of Kamuro-yama (Geospatial Information Authority of Japan)] * [https://www.city.shinjo.yamagata.jp/k001/020/050/150/20150313172806.html Kamuro-yama Shelter Information (Shinjō City Official Website)] * [http://www.ecopolis.or.jp/kanko/ Mogami Regional Tourism Council] {{200 Famous Japanese Mountains}} {{100 Famous Tōhoku Mountains}} {{Mountain-stub|pref=Akita Prefecture|pref2=Yamagata Prefecture}} [[Category:Mountain ranges in Japan]] [[Category:Mountains of Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Mountains of Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:Scenic spots in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Scenic spots in Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinjō City]] [[Category:Geography of Yuzawa City]] [[Category:Kaneyama, Yamagata]] [[Category:Mountains of the Ōu Range]] [[Category:1000-meter mountains]] [[Category:Kamuro-yama]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kamuro-yama-qq]] [[Category:Kamuro-yama]][[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[ja:神室山]] [[Category:Omonoimi faith]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] {{Omonoimi Faith}}

{{Infobox mountain | Name = Kamuro-yama (神室山) | Image = [[File:Kamuroyama.jpg|300px]] | Caption = Kamuro-yama and an emergency shelter (August 2011) | Elevation = 1365.2m | Coordinates = {{Coord|38|54|8|N|140|29|32.3|E|type:mountain_region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Location = {{flag|Japan}}
{{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県}} (Yuzawa City), {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}} (Shinjō City, Kaneyama Town) | Range = Kamuro Range | Type = Fault-block mountain ([[Granite]]) | First Ascent = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|38.902230|140.492306}}|caption=|width=300}}Location of Kamuro-yama }} '''Kamuro-yama''' (神室山) is a mountain that straddles the borders of {{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県}} (Yuzawa City) and {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}} (Shinjō City and Kaneyama Town), serving as the main peak of the Kamuro Range. It is one of the '''{{ill|200 Famous Japanese Mountains|ja|日本二百名山}}'''. This article also describes the Kamuro Range (Kamuro Mountain Range) in general. == Overview == The Kamuro Range, with Kamuro-yama as its main peak, stretches for over 30 km, comprising peaks such as {{ill|Kuro-mori|ja|黒森}} (1,057 m), {{ill|Suishō-mori|ja|水晶森}} (1,097 m), {{ill|Mae-Kamuro-yama|ja|前神室山}} (1,342 m), Kamuro-yama (1,365 m), {{ill|Tengu-mori|ja|天狗森}} (1,302 m), {{ill|Komata-yama|ja|小又山}} (1,366 m), {{ill|Hiuchi-dake|ja|火打岳}} (1,237 m), {{ill|Hachihori-yama|ja|八森山}} (1,098 m), {{ill|Mokuzō-yama|ja|杢蔵山}} (1,026 m), and {{ill|Ōmori-yama|ja|大森山}} (591 m). Although not exceptionally tall, its rugged appearance, sculpted by heavy snowfall, has earned it the nickname "The Mini-Alps of Tōhoku." The entire range is part of the {{ill|Kurikoma Quasi-National Park|ja|栗駒国定公園}}. Kamuro-yama has been a site of {{ill|mountain worship|ja|山岳信仰}} since ancient times, comparable to {{ill|Mount Chōkai|ja|鳥海山}} as a sacred mountain for ascetic practices. According to legend, it was opened by {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂}} during his eastern expeditions. The mountain has strong ties to Mount Chōkai, sharing the same deity, {{ill|Ōmonoimi no Kami|ja|大物忌神}}. It was revered as a deity of water and agriculture, with people climbing the mountain to pray for abundant harvests. Stone markers inscribed with names like "Kamuro Gongen," "Raijin," "Ōta-no-Kami," and "Suijin" remain on the summit, along with several sites thought to have been used for rituals, such as scattering rice to pray for bountiful harvests. Legends like "The Tengu of Kamuro-yama" also abound. Traces of pilgrim accommodations remain in the surrounding villages, such as Yamazaki Warabō in Kaneyama Town. Temples in the region also reflect the mountain's significance in {{ill|Shugendō|ja|修験道}}. Although Shugendō practices at Kamuro-yama declined due to concerns over moral corruption among monks, the mountain remains a site of local reverence. Despite its moderate elevation, heavy snowfall allows alpine plants to thrive at lower altitudes. Well-maintained trails make it a popular destination for hikers. == Hiking Information == * The emergency shelter at the summit was closed due to deterioration but was rebuilt over two years starting in 2009. It reopened in October 2010. A water source is located near the shelter. * Other water sources are limited to a few locations: near the Kamuro-yama shelter, Mokuzō Mountain Lodge, and the trail junction at "Sariguchi" between Komata-yama and Hiuchi-dake. * The ridges are narrow, posing a high risk of slipping. * The annual opening of the Kamuro Range in Shinjō City takes place in early June. [[File:Kamurosan Santyô.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Summit of Kamuro-yama (August 2011)]] == Access == === Akita Prefecture (Yuzawa City) === * '''Yakunai Trailhead''' ** '''Nishinokuchi Route''': This route follows Nishinokuchi River to the summit, featuring stone statues of Fudō Myōō, steep climbs like "Munazuka Hatchō," and scenic wetlands at Onta-no-Kami. ** '''Panorama Route''': This route passes Mae-Kamuro-yama and joins the Araya Route, featuring Kabutayama Shrine and scenic viewpoints. === Yamagata Prefecture (Kaneyama Town) === * '''Suishō-mori Trailhead''': Once a major route through the {{ill|Dewa Province|ja|出羽国}}, this trail leads to Suishō-mori and Mae-Kamuro-yama. * '''Araya Trailhead''': A route along Ōtaki Gorge leading to the summit. === Yamagata Prefecture (Shinjō City) === * '''Donai Trailhead''': This route joins the Kamazawa Trail and ascends via the Daiyama Ridge. * '''Sariguchi Trailhead''': A path through Donai Gorge to Sariguchi. * '''Hiuchi Shinmichi Trailhead''': Directly ascends Hiuchi-dake. * '''Hagino Trailhead''': A route to Hachihori-yama via Ippaimori. * '''San’ya Trailhead''': Passes through Mokuzō-yama and connects to other ridge trails. === Yamagata Prefecture (Mogami Town) === * '''Yakushihara Trailhead''': Ascends to Hachihori-yama. * '''Oyagurumi Trailhead''': Leads to the ridge between Komata-yama and Hachihori-yama. * '''Nishinokuchi Trailhead''': Starts near Shirakawa Dam and ascends Komata-yama. == Gallery == 三十三尋の滝２.jpg|Sanju-sanjihiro Falls on the Nishinokuchi Route 胸突八丁坂２.jpg|Munazuka Hatchō slope on the Nishinokuchi Route クマのひるね坂から御田の神.jpg|Onta-no-Kami seen from Kuma no Hirune slope 前神室山と雄勝・秋ノ宮の集落２.jpg|Mae-Kamuro-yama with Ogatsu and Akinomiya settlements 神室山山頂２.jpg|Summit of Kamuro-yama 避難小屋と雲海の広がる新庄盆地２.jpg|Shelter and sea of clouds over the Shinjō Basin (Gassan in the background) 栗駒山と虎毛山２.jpg|{{ill|Mount Kurikoma|ja|栗駒山}} (left) and {{ill|Torage-yama|ja|虎毛山}} (right) 前神室山.jpg|Mae-Kamuro-yama == Supplementary Notes == * Nearby is another mountain called {{ill|Karekake-dake|ja|禿岳}}, also pronounced "Kamuro-san." According to local lore, the two are considered sisters. * Surrounding sacred mountains include Kaneyama's {{ill|Ryūma-yama|ja|竜馬山}}, Mogami's Gongen-yama, Shinjō's Hachihori-yama, and the "Rokushō-dake" group in the {{ill|Kammu Prefectural Nature Park|ja|加無山県立自然公園}}. == External Links == * [https://maps.gsi.go.jp/#15/38.902230/140.492306/&base=std&ls=std&disp=1&vs=c1j0h0k0l0u0t0z0r0s0m0f1 Topographic Map of Kamuro-yama (Geospatial Information Authority of Japan)] * [https://www.city.shinjo.yamagata.jp/k001/020/050/150/20150313172806.html Kamuro-yama Shelter Information (Shinjō City Official Website)] * [http://www.ecopolis.or.jp/kanko/ Mogami Regional Tourism Council] {{200 Famous Japanese Mountains}} {{100 Famous Tōhoku Mountains}} {{Mountain-stub|pref=Akita Prefecture|pref2=Yamagata Prefecture}} [[Category:Mountain ranges in Japan]] [[Category:Mountains of Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Mountains of Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:Scenic spots in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Scenic spots in Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinjō City]] [[Category:Geography of Yuzawa City]] [[Category:Kaneyama, Yamagata]] [[Category:Mountains of the Ōu Range]] [[Category:1000-meter mountains]] [[Category:Kamuro-yama]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kamuro-yama-qq]] [[Category:Kamuro-yama]][[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[ja:神室山]] [[Category:Omonoimi faith]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] {{Omonoimi Faith}}

revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2023年8月}} {{Infobox 山 |名称 = 神室山 |画像 = [[File:Kamuroyama.jpg|300px]] |画像キャプション = 神室山と避難小屋 (2011年8月) |標高 = 1365.2 |座標 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|38|54|8|N|140|29|32.3|E|type:mountain_region:JP|display=inline,title}} |所在地 = {{JPN}}
[[秋田県]][[湯沢市]]・
[[山形県]][[新庄市]]・[[金山町 (山形県)|金山町]] |山系 = 神室連峰 |種類 = 構造山地（[[花崗岩]]） |初登頂 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|38.902230|140.492306}}|caption=|width=300}}神室山の位置 }} '''神室山'''（かむろさん）は[[秋田県]][[湯沢市]]と[[山形県]][[新庄市]]、[[金山町 (山形県)|金山町]]の県境･市境上にそびえる、神室連峰の主峰となる山である。 『'''[[日本二百名山]]'''』のひとつに数えられる。 この項では、便宜的に神室連峰（神室山地）全般についても記述する。 == 山の概要 == 神室山を主峰とする神室連峰は、黒森(1,057m)から、水晶森(1,097m)、前神室山(1,342m)、神室山(1,365m)、天狗森(1,302m)、小又山(1,366m)、火打岳(1,237m)、八森山(1,098m)、[[杢蔵山]](1,026m)、大森山(591m)と、脊梁が30kmあまりも続く[[山脈]]であり、標高こそ高くはないものの豪雪に磨かれた山容は険しく、そのことから別名「'''東北のミニ[[アルプス山脈|アルプス]]'''」「'''みちのくのアルプス'''」と呼ばれる{{Cite web |title=【募集終了】東北のミニアルプス『神室山』を満喫できる農泊体験 |url=https://yamagatayama.com/news/cat_03/%E3%80%90%E5%8F%82%E5%8A%A0%E8%80%85%E5%8B%9F%E9%9B%86%E3%80%91%E6%9D%B1%E5%8C%97%E3%81%AE%E3%83%9F%E3%83%8B%E3%82%A2%E3%83%AB%E3%83%97%E3%82%B9%E3%80%8E%E7%A5%9E%E5%AE%A4%E5%B1%B1%E3%80%8F%E3%82%92/ |website=やまがた山（山形県山岳情報ポータルサイト） |access-date=2025-02-18 |language=ja}}{{Cite web |url=https://www.pref.yamagata.jp/documents/4081/tourism_kamuro.pdf |title=神室ダム［ダム湖とブナ原生林が織り成す自然景観］ |access-date=2025年2月19日 |publisher=[[山形県]]}}{{Cite web |title=神室山 |url=https://yamagatayama.com/hyakumeizan/no-028/ |website=やまがた山（山形県山岳情報ポータルサイト） |access-date=2025-02-18 |language=ja}}{{Cite web |title=神室山 |url=https://kanko-mogami.jp/spot/mt-kamuro/ |website=【公式】最上地域の観光情報サイト - AMAZINGMOGAMI |access-date=2025-02-18 |language=ja}}。全山[[栗駒国定公園]]に指定されている。 神室山は、古来から[[山岳信仰]]の山として知られ、修験の山として[[鳥海山]]と並び称される存在であった。伝説によれば、[[坂上田村麻呂]]が東征のおりに開山したと伝えられている。鳥海山との結びつきが強く、祭神も同じ[[大物忌神]]である。また、神室山は水や[[農業]]の神として敬われ、人々は山に登っては[[五穀]]豊穣を祈っていた。山頂には「神室権現」、「雷神」、「太田神」、「水神」といった石碑が残されており、山中の窪地を[[田|田んぼ]]に見立て、米などを撒いて五穀豊穣を祈った「御田」の跡と推定される場所が何箇所か残っている。また、「神室山の[[天狗]]」など多くの伝承が言い伝えられている。神室山麓の各集落には、それぞれに神室山の参詣者を迎えた[[宿坊]]の跡とされる場所が残されている。例えば、金山町の山崎藁坊はその一つである。また、金山町内に残る[[寺]]は、神室山の山岳修験にちなんでいる。神室山の[[修験道]]自体は、僧坊に女を囲うなど、破戒行為が著しいことを理由に禁止され、廃れて行ったが、近隣の住民の信仰の対象とされ続けている。 他の山脈に比べて特別標高が高いわけではないが、積雪の多さから比較的低標高に[[高山植物]]が群生しており、[[登山道]]も比較的整備されているため、人気のある登山コースとなっている。 == 登山情報 == * 山頂にあった[[山小屋|避難小屋]]は、老朽化のため使用禁止になっていた。[[2008年]]9月、利用者の要望が強まったことにより、[[2009年]]度から2か年かけて、避難小屋の全面改築を行うことを決めた。[http://yamagata-np.jp/news/200809/30/kj_2008093000474.php]2010年10月に避難小屋は完成し、利用できるようになっている。避難小屋の近くには水場がある。 * 水場は、縦走ルート上には、神室山避難小屋と杢蔵山荘、小又山と火打岳の間にある登山道の分岐点、「砂利口」から少し下った場所にしかない。 * 尾根は痩せているので、滑落の危険性が高い。 * 新庄市の神室連峰山開きは、毎年6月上旬の週末に行われる。 [[ファイル:Kamurosan Santyô.jpg|thumb|250px|right|神室山山頂（2011年8月）]] == アクセス == * 秋田県湯沢市 ** '''役内口'''　 *** 西の又ルート（西の又川に沿って神室山頂上を目指すルート。途中に不動明王の石仏がある最後の水場、胸突八丁の急登、池塘やお花畑の御田ノ神（おんたのかみ）がある） *** パノラマルート（前神室山を通り、有屋口からのルートと合流後、国定公園のレリーフと石像がある鏑山神社（レリーフピーク）を通過するルート） * 山形県金山町 ** '''水晶森口'''（かつて出羽を縦貫するメインルートであった[[有屋峠]]のルート。稜線上に出て、水晶森～前神室山を経由する） ** '''有屋口'''（大滝沿いの登山道から神室山頂上を目指す。山形県側からの代表的な登山ルート） ** '''蒲沢口'''（権八小屋跡で土内口からの登山道と合流し、台山尾根から神室山頂上を目指すルート） * 山形県新庄市 ** '''土内口'''（権八小屋跡で蒲沢口からの登山道と合流し、台山尾根から神室山頂上を目指すルート） ** '''砂利押沢口'''（土内から土内渓谷を通って、砂利口に出るルート。途中に水場がある） ** '''火打新道口'''（土内から火打岳を直接登るルート） ** '''萩野口'''（萩野から一杯森を通って、八森山を目指すルート） ** '''山屋口'''（[[杢蔵山]]を経由し、尾根を縦走するルート。登山道入口に新庄市営山屋キャンプ場、前杢蔵に[[山小屋]]「杢蔵山荘」あり） * 山形県[[最上町]] ** '''薬師原口'''（薬師原から八森山を目指すルート） ** '''親倉見口'''（親倉見から八森山と小又山との間にある稜線、槍が先を目指すルート） ** '''西の又口'''（白川ダムから延びる西の又林道の終点から、小又山を目指すルート） == ギャラリー == 三十三尋の滝２.jpg|西ノ又コースの三十三尋の滝 胸突八丁坂２.jpg|西ノ又コースの胸突八丁坂 クマのひるね坂から御田の神.jpg|クマのひるね坂から御田の神 前神室山と雄勝・秋ノ宮の集落２.jpg|前神室山と雄勝・秋ノ宮の集落 神室山山頂２.jpg|神室山山頂 避難小屋と雲海の広がる新庄盆地２.jpg|避難小屋と[[雲海]]広がる[[新庄盆地]](左奥は[[月山]]) 栗駒山と虎毛山２.jpg|[[栗駒山]]（左奥）と[[虎毛山]]（右手前） 前神室山.jpg|前神室山 == 補足 == * 神室山の近くにはもう一つ、[[奥羽山脈]]に属する[[禿岳]]（小鏑山）がありこちらも「かむろだけ」「かむろさん」と呼ばれている。同じ呼び名であることから、両山は姉妹であるとされており、山麓では姉妹神の争いの伝承が伝えられている。禿岳を区別のため「はげかむろ」と呼ぶ人もいる。 * 前述の通り、神室山は山岳信仰の山であったが、その周囲にも山岳信仰の山は多い。金山町の「[[竜馬山_(山形県)|竜馬山]]」、最上町の「権現山」、神室連峰の1峰でもある新庄市の八森山、「甑岳」と総称される[[加無山県立自然公園]]などである。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == * [https://maps.gsi.go.jp/#15/38.902230/140.492306/&base=std&ls=std&disp=1&vs=c1j0h0k0l0u0t0z0r0s0m0f1 地図閲覧サービス 2万5千分1地形図名：神室山 北東] * [https://www.city.shinjo.yamagata.jp/k001/020/050/150/20150313172806.html 神室山避難小屋の利用について(新庄市ホームページ)] * [http://www.ecopolis.or.jp/kanko/ 最上地域観光協議会] {{日本二百名山}} {{東北百名山}} {{Mountain-stub|pref=秋田県|pref2=山形県}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かむろさん}} [[Category:山岳名目録]] [[Category:山形県の山]] [[Category:秋田県の山]] [[Category:山形県の自然景勝地]] [[Category:秋田県の自然景勝地]] [[Category:新庄市]] [[Category:湯沢市の地理]] [[Category:金山町 (山形県)]] [[Category:奥羽山脈の山]] [[Category:1000メートル峰]]

{{Infobox mountain | Name = Kamuro-yama (神室山) | Image = [[File:Kamuroyama.jpg|300px]] | Caption = Kamuro-yama and an emergency shelter (August 2011) | Elevation = 1365.2m | Coordinates = {{Coord|38|54|8|N|140|29|32.3|E|type:mountain_region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Location = {{flag|Japan}}
{{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県}} (Yuzawa City), {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}} (Shinjō City, Kaneyama Town) | Range = Kamuro Range | Type = Fault-block mountain ([[Granite]]) | First Ascent = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|38.902230|140.492306}}|caption=|width=300}}Location of Kamuro-yama }} '''Kamuro-yama''' (神室山) is a mountain that straddles the borders of {{ill|Akita Prefecture|ja|秋田県}} (Yuzawa City) and {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}} (Shinjō City and Kaneyama Town), serving as the main peak of the Kamuro Range. It is one of the '''{{ill|200 Famous Japanese Mountains|ja|日本二百名山}}'''. This article also describes the Kamuro Range (Kamuro Mountain Range) in general. == Overview == The Kamuro Range, with Kamuro-yama as its main peak, stretches for over 30 km, comprising peaks such as {{ill|Kuro-mori|ja|黒森}} (1,057 m), {{ill|Suishō-mori|ja|水晶森}} (1,097 m), {{ill|Mae-Kamuro-yama|ja|前神室山}} (1,342 m), Kamuro-yama (1,365 m), {{ill|Tengu-mori|ja|天狗森}} (1,302 m), {{ill|Komata-yama|ja|小又山}} (1,366 m), {{ill|Hiuchi-dake|ja|火打岳}} (1,237 m), {{ill|Hachihori-yama|ja|八森山}} (1,098 m), {{ill|Mokuzō-yama|ja|杢蔵山}} (1,026 m), and {{ill|Ōmori-yama|ja|大森山}} (591 m). Although not exceptionally tall, its rugged appearance, sculpted by heavy snowfall, has earned it the nickname "The Mini-Alps of Tōhoku." The entire range is part of the {{ill|Kurikoma Quasi-National Park|ja|栗駒国定公園}}. Kamuro-yama has been a site of {{ill|mountain worship|ja|山岳信仰}} since ancient times, comparable to {{ill|Mount Chōkai|ja|鳥海山}} as a sacred mountain for ascetic practices. According to legend, it was opened by {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂}} during his eastern expeditions. The mountain has strong ties to Mount Chōkai, sharing the same deity, {{ill|Ōmonoimi no Kami|ja|大物忌神}}. It was revered as a deity of water and agriculture, with people climbing the mountain to pray for abundant harvests. Stone markers inscribed with names like "Kamuro Gongen," "Raijin," "Ōta-no-Kami," and "Suijin" remain on the summit, along with several sites thought to have been used for rituals, such as scattering rice to pray for bountiful harvests. Legends like "The Tengu of Kamuro-yama" also abound. Traces of pilgrim accommodations remain in the surrounding villages, such as Yamazaki Warabō in Kaneyama Town. Temples in the region also reflect the mountain's significance in {{ill|Shugendō|ja|修験道}}. Although Shugendō practices at Kamuro-yama declined due to concerns over moral corruption among monks, the mountain remains a site of local reverence. Despite its moderate elevation, heavy snowfall allows alpine plants to thrive at lower altitudes. Well-maintained trails make it a popular destination for hikers. == Hiking Information == * The emergency shelter at the summit was closed due to deterioration but was rebuilt over two years starting in 2009. It reopened in October 2010. A water source is located near the shelter. * Other water sources are limited to a few locations: near the Kamuro-yama shelter, Mokuzō Mountain Lodge, and the trail junction at "Sariguchi" between Komata-yama and Hiuchi-dake. * The ridges are narrow, posing a high risk of slipping. * The annual opening of the Kamuro Range in Shinjō City takes place in early June. [[File:Kamurosan Santyô.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Summit of Kamuro-yama (August 2011)]] == Access == === Akita Prefecture (Yuzawa City) === * '''Yakunai Trailhead''' ** '''Nishinokuchi Route''': This route follows Nishinokuchi River to the summit, featuring stone statues of Fudō Myōō, steep climbs like "Munazuka Hatchō," and scenic wetlands at Onta-no-Kami. ** '''Panorama Route''': This route passes Mae-Kamuro-yama and joins the Araya Route, featuring Kabutayama Shrine and scenic viewpoints. === Yamagata Prefecture (Kaneyama Town) === * '''Suishō-mori Trailhead''': Once a major route through the {{ill|Dewa Province|ja|出羽国}}, this trail leads to Suishō-mori and Mae-Kamuro-yama. * '''Araya Trailhead''': A route along Ōtaki Gorge leading to the summit. === Yamagata Prefecture (Shinjō City) === * '''Donai Trailhead''': This route joins the Kamazawa Trail and ascends via the Daiyama Ridge. * '''Sariguchi Trailhead''': A path through Donai Gorge to Sariguchi. * '''Hiuchi Shinmichi Trailhead''': Directly ascends Hiuchi-dake. * '''Hagino Trailhead''': A route to Hachihori-yama via Ippaimori. * '''San’ya Trailhead''': Passes through Mokuzō-yama and connects to other ridge trails. === Yamagata Prefecture (Mogami Town) === * '''Yakushihara Trailhead''': Ascends to Hachihori-yama. * '''Oyagurumi Trailhead''': Leads to the ridge between Komata-yama and Hachihori-yama. * '''Nishinokuchi Trailhead''': Starts near Shirakawa Dam and ascends Komata-yama. == Gallery == 三十三尋の滝２.jpg|Sanju-sanjihiro Falls on the Nishinokuchi Route 胸突八丁坂２.jpg|Munazuka Hatchō slope on the Nishinokuchi Route クマのひるね坂から御田の神.jpg|Onta-no-Kami seen from Kuma no Hirune slope 前神室山と雄勝・秋ノ宮の集落２.jpg|Mae-Kamuro-yama with Ogatsu and Akinomiya settlements 神室山山頂２.jpg|Summit of Kamuro-yama 避難小屋と雲海の広がる新庄盆地２.jpg|Shelter and sea of clouds over the Shinjō Basin (Gassan in the background) 栗駒山と虎毛山２.jpg|{{ill|Mount Kurikoma|ja|栗駒山}} (left) and {{ill|Torage-yama|ja|虎毛山}} (right) 前神室山.jpg|Mae-Kamuro-yama == Supplementary Notes == * Nearby is another mountain called {{ill|Karekake-dake|ja|禿岳}}, also pronounced "Kamuro-san." According to local lore, the two are considered sisters. * Surrounding sacred mountains include Kaneyama's {{ill|Ryūma-yama|ja|竜馬山}}, Mogami's Gongen-yama, Shinjō's Hachihori-yama, and the "Rokushō-dake" group in the {{ill|Kammu Prefectural Nature Park|ja|加無山県立自然公園}}. == External Links == * [https://maps.gsi.go.jp/#15/38.902230/140.492306/&base=std&ls=std&disp=1&vs=c1j0h0k0l0u0t0z0r0s0m0f1 Topographic Map of Kamuro-yama (Geospatial Information Authority of Japan)] * [https://www.city.shinjo.yamagata.jp/k001/020/050/150/20150313172806.html Kamuro-yama Shelter Information (Shinjō City Official Website)] * [http://www.ecopolis.or.jp/kanko/ Mogami Regional Tourism Council] {{200 Famous Japanese Mountains}} {{100 Famous Tōhoku Mountains}} {{Mountain-stub|pref=Akita Prefecture|pref2=Yamagata Prefecture}} [[Category:Mountain ranges in Japan]] [[Category:Mountains of Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Mountains of Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:Scenic spots in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Scenic spots in Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinjō City]] [[Category:Geography of Yuzawa City]] [[Category:Kaneyama, Yamagata]] [[Category:Mountains of the Ōu Range]] [[Category:1000-meter mountains]] [[Category:Kamuro-yama]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Kamuro-yama-qq]] [[Category:Kamuro-yama]][[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[ja:神室山]] [[Category:Omonoimi faith]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] {{Omonoimi Faith}}
